observations.recommendation: Adjust the books of accounts and property records, as warranted, after reconciliation; and

We recommended management that:

     a. Accounts payable are to be recognized by the  Municipal  Accountant
        only  when  the  goods  and  services  are   accepted/rendered   or
        creditor/contractor’s billing statements are received  and  not  on
        the   obligations   recognized   in    the    Registry/Ledger    of
        Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO);


     b. Also, make the necessary adjustment to correct the account balances
        of assets and liability account for the undelivered equipment:


            GF books:
                 Accounts Payable 1,099,000.00
                       Motor Vehicles   1,099,000.00

            SEF books:
                 Accounts Payable 94,400.00
                                  IT            and            Communication
                      Technology Equipment   94,00.00

     c. Subsequent recognition of accounts payment is to be supported  with
        the  necessary  documents  in  order  to  prove  the  validity   of
        obligations.

We recommended that management direct  the  end  users/requisitioning
      offices to prepare and submit to the Accounting Unit the SSMI and RIS
      with the List  of  Recipients  with  their  corresponding  signatures
      acknowledging the receipt of the above items by the recipients.

We recommended that management demand again from the supplier for the
      replacement of the defective bulb, indicating therein the  period  of
      replacement. Liquidated  damages  are  to  be  imposed  in  case  the
      supplier fails to comply within the agreed period. Otherwise, if  the
      supplier continuously failed to  replace  the  defective  bulbs,  the
      management will take over the  replacement  of  the  defective  light
      bulbs.


   Moreover, direct the Municipal  Engineer  to  ensure  that  PPAs  are
      completed within the planned timelines and are  to  be  assessed  and
      evaluated thoroughly, including  the  location  to  ensure  that  all
      possible issues are addressed during  the  planning  stage  to  avoid
      delay in the completion of PPAs. Also, provide additional  funds  for
      additional scope of works due to the new site location.

We recommended that management to direct the Municipal Health Officer
      to include only those front liners/health workers  who  are  eligible
      for subsequent AHDP  and  SRA.  Also,  the  no.  of  days  physically
      reporting for work are to be computed up  to  the  allowable  maximum
      days per month to avoid excess payment thereof.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Budget to include
      the 5% GAD in the supplemental budget either separately or  attributes
      to the PPAs. Also, direct the inclusion in  the  final  Accomplishment
      Report the attributed GAD from the supplemental budgets.


   Moreover, direct the GFPS to prepare and submit for  DILG  endorsement
      any amended/supplemental GPB in case of approved supplemental  budgets
      and  update  the  GAD  budget  attributed  cost  based  on  the  final
      appropriation at  year-end  to  determine  the  actual  percentage  of
      compliance with GAD budget requirement.
Require the Inventory Committee to complete the preparation of RPCPPE and to coordinate with the Accounting Unit for reconciliation, copy furnished our office;
Send our Office invitation letter as witness whenever a physical inventory is to be conducted.

The management to prepare the necessary documents for the transfer/turn-over of the barangay roads to the concerned barangay. These documents will be the basis of the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to drop these assets from the books of accounts.
Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to require the barangay bookkeeper to record these road projects in the barangay books of accounts.

The management to direct the accounting unit to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the collections and remittances, and cash advances and liquidations of the Municipal Treasurer and Collecting Officer, and Disbursing Officers, respectively, to facilitate in the reconciliation of their accountabilities and to determine their correct accountabilities at a given period.

Management to direct the: Municipal Accountant To record purchases in bulk or carried on stock to its appropriate Inventory account on the basis of Delivery Receipts and Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR); and
To record expenses on the basis of the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) together with the supporting RIS.
Requisitioning Office To prepare and submit RIS with its supporting documents to the Supply Officer.
Supply Officer a.  To consolidate the RIS in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit to the Accounting Unit for the recognition of issuance under its appropriate Expenses account.

Management to require the Supply Officer and Inspectorate Team to provide the date of acceptance and inspection, respectively. Also, AIR is to bear all the signatures of the Inspectorate Team. Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to check whether the supporting documents are properly accomplished before approval thereof.

Management directs the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that the procedures provided under Annex H of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 using small value procurement modality be strictly followed. RFQs be sent to three suppliers of known qualifications; meaning suppliers known to be dealers of such goods and are permanently displayed in their respective business establishments. Also, complete names and address of person representing the suppliers who were provided with RFQs must see to it that they are duly signed to determine its authenticity.
Management to assign personnel to be responsible for the following duties:
Inclusion of high value properties to insurance coverage.
Extraction from the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment, the high value properties subject to insurance.
Appraisal of the insurable properties.

Preparation and submission of Property Inventory Form (PIF) listing of all the insurable properties and other assets, showing their latest appraised values/valuation, appraisal date, location, and other information using the form provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2018-002 to COA and to GIF, GSIS, not later than April 30 of each year.
Inclusion in the LGU Annual Budget the amount of premiums for the general insurance covering all insurable properties and ensure its payment to the GSIS.

Management to direct the MPDO and Municipal Engineer to submit a list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented at the start of the year. Moreover, require them to prepare monitoring report of PPAs monthly and immediately submit the same to our Office.

Management to inform COA within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity so that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices and already posted for validation purposes.
